title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S5
body:
**Scene 5: 04:00–05:30 · The Radio**

HERO FRAME: A 1949 wooden console radio in the corner of the parlor · its dial illuminated with a soft amber glow · Walter's hand on the tuning knob (only hand visible) · the speaker grille glowing faintly · books and a clock on top of the cabinet

MOTION: kling — the dial knob turns slowly under the hand. The amber glow of the dial pulses gently as he passes through frequencies. Faint white-noise visible as a soft static shimmer over the speaker grille.

MOOD: Routine of hope.

AUDIO: A vintage tube radio hums and crackles. Walter twists the dial. Static. *"Nothing for sixteen days. I keep turning it on. I keep turning the dial. I do not know why."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
